title: Derive Meshtastic gateway queue capacity regression from Kconfig
description: Follow-up from project-LICHEN-t2hn.6.4 reviews: meshtastic_adapter tests verify the gateway default queue fits heartbeat + full sync + default NodeDB peers, but the gateway queue depth constants and static-sync record count are duplicated in the test. Make this drift-proof by deriving the queue settings from Kconfig/build metadata or adding compile-time assertions/shared test-visible constants.
notes:
Implemented no-hardware drift-proof gateway queue-capacity regression. Added hidden LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE_QUEUE_DEPTH_MIN in lichen/apps/gateway/Kconfig and used it as the lower bound for LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE_QUEUE_DEPTH. Added test-local lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter/Kconfig to source gateway Kconfig without enabling the BLE stack. Exposed LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_* sync record-count macros in adapter.h, tied adapter.c static config-section count to those macros with BUILD_ASSERT, and updated meshtastic_adapter full-sync tests to use the generated minimum legal gateway queue capacity plus shared full-sync record count.

Explorer/test-design: Hegel mapped gateway queue depth CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE_QUEUE_DEPTH, runtime ble_meshtastic_from_radio_capacity(), NodeDB peer cap CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_NODEDB_MAX_PEERS, and recommended shared record-count macros with build asserts. Banach identified duplicated test literals 20/20/14 and recommended deriving the gateway capacity from Kconfig and record count from shared adapter capacity contract, plus preserving the minimum legal capacity invariant.

Codereview: Goodall initially found P2 that checking only selected/default queue depth could miss an undersized legal lower bound if default rises but range minimum stays low. Fixed by adding LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE_QUEUE_DEPTH_MIN and asserting that minimum fits heartbeat + full sync. Goodall re-review: no P0-P2. Copernicus review/re-review: no P0-P2. Bacon third review after the subsystem-only fix: no P0-P2.

Local checks: git diff --check passed after final changes.

EC2 validation: launched i-0f048c8e6628fcd0c in us-east-2a with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr. Primary EBS worktree was dirty/behind, so validation used clean worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-p2pu-validate from origin/main plus local patch. Bootstrap warning: awscli package unavailable on Ubuntu noble; installed host build prerequisites because gcc was missing. Initial stale Twister run twister-out-p2pu used an earlier patch that enabled CONFIG_LORA_LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_BLE in the subsystem test; it failed native_sim due Zephyr Bluetooth userchan -Werror=stringop-overflow and qemu_x86 unexpected eof. Fixed by making the minimum queue symbol independent of BLE and removing BT/BLE enables from the subsystem test.

Passing EC2 command: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_adapter -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --outdir twister-out-p2pu-rerun --inline-logs --extra-args ZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen. Result: 2 scenarios / 4 instances selected; 3 passed; 1 static skip; 0 failed; 0 errored; 0 warnings; 163 test cases executed; 31 skipped; duration 82.40s. Sizes: native_sim meshtastic_gateway_adapter text=245268 data=7135 bss=74924 dec=327327; native_sim meshtastic_adapter text=112162 data=1473 bss=40310 dec=153945; qemu_x86 meshtastic_adapter text=146680 data=4868 bss=115272 dec=266820.

EC2 cleanup complete: sync run, /mnt/lichen-zephyr unmounted, vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 detached and available, instance i-0f048c8e6628fcd0c terminated, temporary security group sg-0f12734b06a11329d deleted.
close_reason: Completed drift-proof Kconfig-derived queue-capacity regression with reviews and EC2 Twister evidence
